core_mechanism: finite-rank feedback stabilization of an infinite-dimensional dissipative evolution equation, in which a control built from finitely many localized actuators via oblique projection onto a finite-dimensional subspace forces exponential (mean-square) decay of noise-driven perturbations
brief_reason: The abstract builds a feedback law as a finite linear combination of localized actuators via oblique projections onto finite-dimensional subspaces and, by tuning actuator configuration and feedback strength, drives exponential mean-square (and almost-sure) decay of the stochastic parabolic dynamics.
